behavior_summary: This script implements a "stepping stone" world item that responds to hits and U8P_LAVASINK::release interactions by running a multi-stage visual/state transition (push/pop of tiles, frame/type changes and SFX).

U8P_LAVASINK::gotHit is the hit handler: it only proceeds when the item's frame equals 0x08 and then inspects the item's quality to decide behavior. For certain quality values (quality == 1 or quality >= 100) it runs the transition; when an adjacent item's quality equals this item's quality+1 it will spawn a complementary transform (U8P_LAVASINK::func0374) on that neighbor.

U8P_LAVASINK::release handles a player U8P_LAVASINK::release interaction: when the item's quality is in a mid-range (10..99) it may either grant AccumulateDexterity(0x0F) and then invoke the same transition sequence (U8P_LAVASINK::func01E0) or set local guards that prevent the award. It suspends while the effect sequence runs.
U8P_LAVASINK::func01E0 orchestrates the animation: read frame, playSFX, call transform helper(s) (U8P_LAVASINK::func022D then U8P_LAVASINK::func04E8) and suspend between stages.

U8P_LAVASINK::func022D and U8P_LAVASINK::func0374 perform the core push/pop animation steps. They temporarily change item type (0x02F3), set/advance/decrement frames, push this and eligible ethereal neighbors, spawn a FREE:: helper (FREE::func1C19) to schedule timing/cleanup, suspend, and repeat up to a bounded number of cycles (appears <= 8). U8P_LAVASINK::func0374 pops this item at Z+1 in its main loop (inverse direction) while U8P_LAVASINK::func022D uses base Z pops; both restore ethereal neighbors afterwards.

U8P_LAVASINK::func04E8 finalizes the transition: sets a different temporary type (0x02FB) for this item, resets frame to 0, pops it into place, and iterates popped ethereal items to call Item::fall on them as cleanup.
U8P_LAVASINK::look simply returns the short player-facing description 'stepping stone '.
U8P_LAVASINK::enterFastArea and U8P_LAVASINK::leaveFastArea hooks are present but decompiled as no-ops (no recovered logic).
notable_control_flow: U8P_LAVASINK::gotHit: early guard requires Item::getFrame(this) == 0x08; if false the hit handler returns without running the effect.

U8P_LAVASINK::gotHit: conditional branches on Item::getQuality(this): special handling when quality == 1 or quality >= 100; for quality >= 100, checks a nearby item and may spawn U8P_LAVASINK::func0374 on that neighbor if its quality-1 equals this item's quality.
U8P_LAVASINK::release: only proceeds when quality is in 10..99; inside that branch local flags determine whether AccumulateDexterity(0x0F) is awarded and whether U8P_LAVASINK::func01E0 is invoked.
U8P_LAVASINK::func01E0 sequences effects with suspend points: playSFX, call U8P_LAVASINK::func022D, suspend, call U8P_LAVASINK::func04E8, suspend — allowing asynchronous visual progression.

U8P_LAVASINK::func022D/U8P_LAVASINK::func0374: each runs a bounded loop (local3 from 1 to <=8) that pushes/pops items, spawns FREE::func1C19 with different small args, and suspends between iterations. Both collect eligible ethereal neighbors and later pop/restore them.
spawn calls schedule FREE::func1C19 (cleanup/timer) with different numeric args (observed 0x08 and 0x05 variants) depending on the transform helper, which affects timing/variant behavior.
open_questions: Precise semantic meaning of the various Item::quality numeric ranges (1, 10..99, >=100) — quality appears to encode state or linked-pair relationships but exact mapping is inferred.
Exact role and final meaning of temporary type ids 0x02F3 and 0x02FB used during transform sequences (likely temporary visual/state markers but exact gameplay semantics unknown).
Origin and semantics of the 'local2' value tested in U8P_LAVASINK::release (its source is not present in the decompiled excerpt).
Why spawn arguments to FREE::func1C19 differ between U8P_LAVASINK::func022D and U8P_LAVASINK::func0374 (0x08 vs 0x05 observed) — likely controls timing or variant cleanup behavior but not confirmed.
Meaning of ethereal item type 0x0199 (excluded from pushes/pops) — what items it represents in the game world.

main_function.name: U8P_LAVASINK::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle when this stepping-stone item is struck: conditionally trigger a multi-stage tile/item transform animation and possibly spawn a complementary transform on a neighboring item based on frame and quality checks.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the item receives a hit (U8P_LAVASINK::gotHit event).
Only proceeds if Item::getFrame(this) == 0x08 (guarded early in the function).
Further gated by Item::getQuality(this) values (special behavior when quality == 1 or quality >= 100; adjacency-based spawn when a neighbor's quality-1 equals this quality).

function_map.name: U8P_LAVASINK::gotHit
function_map.role: Event handler for hits that triggers the stepping-stone transform sequence when frame/quality conditions match.

function_map.name: U8P_LAVASINK::release
function_map.role: Player 'U8P_LAVASINK::release' interaction: gates on mid-range quality, may award AccumulateDexterity and invoke the shared effect sequence (U8P_LAVASINK::func01E0).

function_map.name: U8P_LAVASINK::look
function_map.role: Look/inspect handler that returns the short description 'stepping stone '.

function_map.name: U8P_LAVASINK::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry hook present but decompiled as no-op / no recovered logic.

function_map.name: U8P_LAVASINK::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit hook present but decompiled as no-op / no recovered logic.

function_map.name: U8P_LAVASINK::func01E0
function_map.role: High-level effect sequencer: play SFX, call transform helpers (U8P_LAVASINK::func022D then U8P_LAVASINK::func04E8) and suspend between stages to animate the transition.

function_map.name: U8P_LAVASINK::func022D
function_map.role: Transform helper that pushes/pops this item and eligible ethereal neighbors, sets temporary type/frame, advances frames, spawns FREE::func1C19, and repeats up to a bounded number of cycles (one direction of the stepping-stone animation).

function_map.name: U8P_LAVASINK::func0374
function_map.role: Complementary transform helper to U8P_LAVASINK::func022D that adjusts frames in the opposite direction and pops this item at Z+1 during animation cycles; spawns FREE::func1C19 for timing/cleanup.

function_map.name: U8P_LAVASINK::func04E8
function_map.role: Finalizer helper that sets a different temporary type/frame, pops this item into final position, and pops/falls any previously pushed ethereal neighbors as cleanup.

internal_calls.purpose: Invoke the high-level effect/animation sequence after frame/quality checks.
Spawn the complementary transform on an adjacent item when that neighbor's quality-1 equals this item's quality (adjacency-linked transition).
Run the same effect sequence on U8P_LAVASINK::release when conditions (quality range and local flags) allow.
Start the first stage of the transform/animation (type/frame change and push/pop behavior).
Complete the transform/animation with final type/frame set and cleanup of ethereal neighbors.

external_calls.purpose: Read the item's current frame to gate hit behavior (guard requires frame == 0x08).
Read this item's quality to decide whether to run transitions or spawn complementary transforms on neighbors.
Gate U8P_LAVASINK::release behavior on quality being within 10..99.
Award or accumulate dexterity (0x0F) when U8P_LAVASINK::release conditions indicate success.
Play a sound effect to accompany the transition/animation.
Temporarily change this item's type to 0x02F3 for animation staging.
Set or advance frames to animate the item during the transform.
Temporarily place this item or nearby ethereal items on the world stack prior to pop operations during animation.
Place/move this item or ethereal neighbors at computed world coordinates (getZ/getY/getX) as part of animation.
Retrieve ethereal/top neighbor items for potential inclusion in push/pop cycles.
Spawn FREE::func1C19 for timing/cleanup during the transform; used to schedule asynchronous helper tasks.
Temporarily change this item's type to 0x02F3 for the inverse-direction animation.
Pop this item at Z+1 during the main animation loop (raises or lowers it by one level depending on direction).
Set temporary type 0x02FB for final placement during the finishing stage.
Make popped ethereal neighbors fall/settle as part of cleanup.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a "stepping stone" world item that responds to hits and U8P_LAVASINK::release interactions by running a multi-stage visual/state transition (push/pop of tiles, frame/type changes and SFX).
